per_video.my_assessment:
SURVIVING CLIP — I disagree with the 9/10. Human score ~4-5/10. The judged text was only "Y prime is the derivative of y."; the shipped clip adds a never-judged sentence containing garbled ASR ("dy dx = m * x to the n -1" — wrong coefficient letter, viewer sees a factually wrong power rule) and ends on a dangling verse fragment ("applications of Divives apply." = ASR mangling of 'applications of derivatives'). Title ("Area Under the Curve") mismatches content (derivative definition). Role=definition is correct. Card absent but arguably needed ("this is a calculus parody song") — without it a viewer is confused why there's [Music] mid-equation. Understandable in isolation? Barely — the definition lands, the rest is noise. Ship-worthy only after trimming to sentence 8.

REJECTIONS (5+ examined): The dominant pattern is a CATEGORY ERROR: detection called a parody song "lecture" (0.9) so worked-problem gates (missing_problem_statement/missing_reasoning/missing_result) were applied to song verses. c_u0012 and the c_u0008/9/10 span (the "I'm just a constant, nobody loves me... call it C" verse) are the video's most clippable, self-contained entertainment moments — a viewer needs no 'problem statement' to enjoy them — OVER-REJECTED by the wrong contract. c_u0015 (limits verse) borderline over-rejected for the same reason. c_u0001/c_u0002 and c_u0005 rightly rejected AS BOUNDED: repair had inflated them to 138-175s multi-verse sprawls that are genuinely incoherent as single clips — but note repair CREATED that sprawl from 10-30s verse anchors; expansion was the only repair lever and it made things worse. Dominant failure kinds: missing_prerequisite (6), missing_problem_statement (6), purpose_identifiable=false gate. Net: yield 1/12 with the true best material killed; correct outcome for this video would be 2-3 short entertainment-cut verses.

per_video.rejections_dump:
6 rejections: 5 repair + 1 dedupe.
1. c_u0003 (rising_action), repair, 11.61-68.97s (57.4s), judge 0.3, kinds=[unresolved_reference x2, missing_prerequisite]; first verdict 2/10 gates topic_identifiable=false, source_grounded=false. Gist: "it's a story in my life... I'm a junior in high school and I want to take calculus... calculus is way more different from algebra than algebra is from arithmetic... I'm handed the calculus book... equations with squiggly lines I had never seen... I said I will never learn this."
2. c_u0008 (claim), repair, 66.66-196.91s (130.2s), judge 0.5, kinds=[unresolved_reference x3]; judge flagged "that fog" as dangling. Gist: full arc — book intimidation -> "the universe is speaking this language" -> week-by-week progress -> "this fog began to dissipate" -> equations became friends -> Mandarin analogy -> "too many people think they got to know something instantly."
3. c_u0009 (resolution), repair, 66.66-219.34s (152.7s), judge 0.4, kinds=[unresolved_reference x2 ("this fog", "these quite equations"), not_source_grounded ("starts mid-discussion")]. Gist: same arc extended to the ending ("don't hesitate to ask for help... I've tutored people... I've been tutored").
4. c_u0002 (claim), repair, 0.0-68.97s (69.0s), judge 0.3, kinds=[not_source_grounded]; verdict 2/10: "starts and ends mid-sentence". Gist: video opens mid-sentence "through it I have a I have a..." then the arithmetic->algebra->calculus riff.
5. c_u0005 (rising_action), repair, 55.62-177.38s (121.8s), judge 0.4, kinds=[unresolved_reference x2 ("that equation", "that the other day"), missing_concept]; verdict 2/10 claims "unclear what the subject is" — though "handed the calculus book" is verbatim in the span.
6. c_u0006 (rising_action), DEDUPE, 55.62-196.91s (141.3s), final_quality=0.855, reason "overlap loser to c_u0007". Its verdict was 6/10, understandable=false, but ALL four hard-core gates true (kept as best-partial), sole kind missing_prerequisite ("assumes calculus notation"). Gist: the complete story from "that's why it's generally not taught in high school" through the moral "the more badly you want to get there the more you will work to dissipate that fog."
per_video.my_assessment:
SURVIVING CLIP — content is genuinely the video's giftable insight (Mandarin analogy for learning hard things) and IS understandable in isolation, but both boundaries are broken: starts mid-clause ("is my measure of what I need to do..." — missing "and that") and ends mid-clause ("...instantly in order" — cut before "to ever be good at it"). boundary_score=1.0 is fictitious: the chunk-fallback sentence index marks every caption chunk ends_with_period=True, so snapping was a no-op and the ends_on_period metric would read perfect on objectively mid-word cuts. The judge saw the exact chopped text and still gave 9/10 with all_references_resolved and source_grounded true — it missed both truncations. Uncredited interviewer crosstalk ("yeah I don't know any of this yeah") sits inside the clip because detection chose story over interview (no speaker handling). Role=intuition is accurate. Card absent; a one-liner ("Tyson on how he learned calculus") would materially help the cold open. Human score ~6/10.

REJECTIONS — the sharpest over-rejection evidence of the three videos: (a) c_u0008: judge flagged "that fog" as an unresolved reference, but the metaphor is INTRODUCED inside the clip ("it was as though this fog began to dissipate") — a factually wrong verdict killed a good 130s span. (b) c_u0005: judge wrote "unclear what the subject is" when "handed the calculus book" is in the text — another hallucinated gap. (c) c_u0003 (the story setup, 57s): compelling, self-contained-enough; first verdict 2/10 "topic unclear" is wrong; OVER-REJECTED (needs a start trim, not a kill). (d) c_u0002: rightly rejected — video literally starts mid-sentence at 0.0s. (e) c_u0006 (dedupe): the full 141s story arc, the best viewer-facing clip of the video, passed all hard gates at 6/10 but lost dedupe to the 42s fragment because the fragment's judge-inflated 9/10 gave it higher final_quality (0.948 vs 0.855) — the containment tie-break optimized for the wrong thing. Dominant kinds: unresolved_reference (7 instances, at least 2 hallucinated), not_source_grounded. Net yield 1/7 with the best material dropped at dedupe.

7 rejections, ALL stage=repair, 5 distinct spans (c_u0004=c_u0006 identical; c_u0003=c_u0005 identical). Every candidate was expanded by the repair loop to 126-294s (39-91% of the whole video) and then failed.
1. c_u0004 & c_u0006 (definition x2), 43.26-324.38s (281.1s — 87% of the video), judge 0.3, kinds=[unresolved_reference x2 ("this", "like this"), missing_prerequisite ("Mathematical notation for limits and derivatives")], gates visuals_sufficient=false. 3. c_u0007 (definition), 162.36-324.38s (162.0s), judge 0.4, kinds=[unresolved_reference ("the previous equation" — NB: that phrase does not occur in the span text), missing_prerequisite ("functions"), missing_prerequisite ("displacement function")]. Gist: derivatives definition + notation + rules list + applications + integration explanation + outro.
4. c_u0002 (explanation), 0.0-126.24s (126.2s), judge 0.3, kinds=[unresolved_reference ("this"), missing_prerequisite (one-to-one/many-to-one), missing_reasoning ("continuity cut off mid-sentence")]. Gist: calculus intro, functions definition, 2D graphing, vertical line test, limits definition, start of continuity.
5. c_u0003 & c_u0005 (claim x2), 0.0-255.48s (255.5s, also > 240s max), judge 0.3-0.4, kinds=[missing_prerequisite x2 ("substitution/simplification", "indeterminate")]. Gist: everything except the outro.
per_video.my_assessment:
The 0-clip outcome is a process failure more than a content failure. Examined all 5 distinct spans:
(1) The whole-video expansions (c_u0004/6 at 281s, c_u0001 at 294s, c_u0003/5 at 255s) are rightly rejected AS SPANS — a 4.7-minute 'clip' of a 5.4-minute video is not a clip, and two of them exceed max_clip_duration_s=240 so they could never ship at that length anyway (repair's CLOSURE_MAX_SPAN_S=300 > shippable 240 means the judge evaluated unshippable text). But repair manufactured these monsters from reasonable 40-50s definition anchors; expansion cannot fix missing_visual or missing-notation complaints, and each expansion added new 'jumps between topics' incoherence.
(2) c_u0002 (0-126s: functions + vertical line test + limits intro) is MOSTLY OVER-REJECTED: the vertical-line-test segment ("make a vertical line through your graph and if it intersects more than one point it is not a function") and the functions definition are fully self-contained even without visuals; a human viewer follows 0-126s fine. Complaints like "assumes algebraic concepts" are over-strict for a video whose stated premise is a Calculus-1 recap.
(3) c_u0007 partially over-rejected: the Riemann-slices integration explanation is followable blind; one flagged reference ("the previous equation") is a judge hallucination — the phrase isn't in the span.
(4) The honest blocker across all candidates is visual deixis ("the general expression of limits is this", "like this") with has_perception=false — the video shows formulas on screen and the pipeline had no on-screen text to feed the judge. With perception (or a card stating the formula), several spans plausibly pass. Dominant kinds: missing_prerequisite (8), unresolved_reference (7, visual-deictic "this"), missing_visual. Right outcome would be 2-3 sub-topic clips (functions/vertical-line-test; derivatives definition; integration-as-slicing); the pipeline offered only whole-video candidates and shipped nothing.

cross_video_findings:
Baseline: frozen structures, live assembly+judge (Gemini keys verified: author gemini-2.5-flash and judge gemini-2.5-flash-lite both returned valid JSON in smoke test). Full per-video JSON dumps with complete span texts, verdicts, gates, and unit inventories are at /private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-vincentfeng-Documents-practice/a2b9f829-d71b-4851-a1da-5586af4521e6/scratchpad/{uqwC41RDPyg,NjvwWiCYLl4,yfajBIaDf1Q}.json (instrumented mirror of run_eval's assembly path: dump_clips.py in the same dir; assemble_clips called with settings=dict(config.DEFAULTS), topic="" since no golden files exist).

1. YIELD COLLAPSE AT ONE GATE. 26 anchor candidates -> 2 shipped clips (7.7%). 23 of 24 ledger entries are stage=repair; 1 is dedupe; snap/post_merge_judge/quality_floor/max_clips rejected NOTHING. The 6-stage rejection ledger is functionally a single judge+repair gate. Yield is being set almost entirely by judge strictness (min_comprehension_score 0.70, config.py:218) plus the hard-core gate in validate.py:377-381.

2. JUDGE IS BIMODAL AND MISCALIBRATED IN BOTH DIRECTIONS. Every rejected span scored 2-4/10; both survivors scored 9/10; the only mid-band verdict (6/10 best-partial) was later killed by dedupe. The judge is over-strict on long spans — "assumes knowledge of calculus/limits/functions" (missing_prerequisite, the #1 kind, ~15 instances) on videos whose titles announce calculus, and worked-problem gates applied to non-problem content — while blind on short ones: both 9/10 survivors have objectively broken text (NjvwWiCYLl4 starts AND ends mid-clause; uqwC41RDPyg ships a never-judged garbled sentence). Failure-kind ranking across all 24 rejections: missing_prerequisite > unresolved_reference > missing_problem_statement > missing_reasoning/missing_result > not_source_grounded/other.

3. JUDGE HALLUCINATIONS ARE LOAD-BEARING. At least 3 rejections rest partly on factually wrong failure reasons: "that fog" flagged dangling though introduced in-clip (NjvwWiCYLl4 c_u0008); "unclear what the subject is" though "handed the calculus book" is verbatim in-span (c_u0005); "the previous equation" flagged though the phrase never occurs in the span (yfajBIaDf1Q c_u0007). The repair loop then chases these phantom gaps by expanding the span.

4. REPAIR-BY-EXPANSION IS THE WRONG LEVER AND ACTIVELY HARMFUL. expand_candidate (validate.py:163-277) only grows spans. Observed: 10-60s anchors inflated to 100-294s (up to CLOSURE_MAX_SPAN_S=300, config.py:213) which then fail for "jumps between topics", and 3 spans exceeded max_clip_duration_s=240 (config.py:123) — the judge evaluated text that could never ship at that length. Expansion cannot fix missing_visual, ASR garble, or "assumes prior knowledge" complaints. There is no trim/shrink repair, so candidates with one bad edge (e.g. NjvwWiCYLl4 c_u0003) die instead of losing a sentence.

5. SHIPPED TEXT != JUDGED TEXT. Min-duration extension at snap adds sentences after judging (refine.py:85 literally warns "content beyond the judged span") but only MERGED spans are re-judged (assemble/__init__.py:113-116). The uqwC41RDPyg survivor's 9/10 applies to one clean sentence; the shipped clip adds a garbled power rule ("dy dx = m * x to the n -1") and a dangling verse fragment. Post-snap extended spans need the same re-judge treatment as merged spans.

6. CHUNK-FALLBACK SENTENCES NEUTRALIZE BOUNDARY QUALITY. sentences_from_chunks fabricates ends_with_period=True for every caption chunk (sentences.py:392). On unpunctuated supadata transcripts (NjvwWiCYLl4) snapping becomes a no-op: the survivor starts mid-clause, ends mid-clause, yet gets boundary_score=1.0 and would count 100% on ends_on_period_rate. Boundary metrics are unmeasurable exactly where boundaries are worst.

7. CONTEXT CARDS NEVER FIRE WHERE NEEDED. Cards are generated only when referential prereqs exist (context_card.py:48-49). Both survivors shipped with empty cards despite cold-open, mid-thought starts where a one-line orientation ("Tyson describing how he learned calculus") is exactly what a feed viewer needs. Zero cards across the entire audit -> carded_rate signal is vacuous on these videos.

8. DOMAIN DETECTION DRIVES CATEGORY-ERROR REJECTIONS. Parody song -> "lecture" (0.9): worked-problem contract gates (missing_problem_statement/reasoning/result) killed the video's best entertainment verses ("I'm just a constant, nobody loves me... call it C"). Tyson interview -> "story" (0.9), not interview: no speaker handling, so interviewer crosstalk ("I'm screaming at you sorry the level sound levels") pollutes spans and sits uncredited inside the surviving clip. Unit-level role labels themselves were mostly accurate (definition/intuition/rising_action fit); the problem is the video-level contract, not unit roles.

9. DEDUPE OPTIMIZES THE WRONG SCORE. Containment tie-break keeps the higher final_quality spec; on NjvwWiCYLl4 the judge-inflated 9/10 on a chopped 42s fragment (final_quality 0.948) beat the complete 141s story arc that passed all hard gates (0.855) — the best viewer-facing clip of the corpus was dropped at dedupe.

10. MISSING PERCEPTION IS A SYSTEMATIC KILL FACTOR. has_perception=false in all three frozen structures (NjvwWiCYLl4 even has perception.json + video.mp4 cached but the frozen structure predates the merge). On the visually-driven yfajBIaDf1Q, every candidate carried visual deixis ("the general expression of limits is this") with ON-SCREEN TEXT "(none available)" — visuals_sufficient=false is then unfixable by any repair, guaranteeing 0 yield.

11. LEDGER DOUBLE-COUNTING. Convergent candidates that expand to identical spans (cache-shared verdicts) each write a Rejection: 24 entries but only ~16 distinct spans (uqwC41RDPyg 11->7, yfajBIaDf1Q 7->5). rejections_repair in eval metrics overstates distinct kills.

12. MINOR: clip titles inherit the TreeSeg unit/node topic and can mismatch clip content ("Calculus Song: Area Under the Curve" on a derivative-definition clip); video_title is empty in structures ("" in all three dumps).

Highest-leverage fixes suggested by this audit, in order: (a) add trim-to-best-subspan as a repair move (and let anchors be judged at sub-unit scale before expansion); (b) re-judge any post-judge span change, not just merges; (c) real sentence boundaries for chunk transcripts (the cached punctuation/ dir exists for two of these videos but the eval sentence path ignores it); (d) generate a card whenever the clip starts mid-thought, not only when referential is non-empty; (e) entertainment/music detection or at minimum stop applying worked-problem gates outside problem roles; (f) dedupe tie-break should prefer complete arcs over judge-score-inflated fragments (e.g. weigh narrative-role coverage).
